recent_activities_14d.subject: [Clari - Email Received] RE: Team Update and Executive Connect
[Clari - Email Sent] Re: Team Update and Executive Connect

our_open_deliverables.text: Send an updated pricing breakdown email removing iRisk and providing line-item unit costs for add-on modules (power users, business users, intake, SaaS integration)
Confirm any data migration size limitations and resource/cost considerations with internal team and communicate options back to Cadence
Review the complex coded template requirement with the product team and propose alternative solutions to support conditional clause logic
Confirm with the product team whether restricted Salesforce access can be configured to allow sales users to view status and signed documents without full Zycus access
Confirm Peppol invoice-processing capability for Belgium/France/Italy and schedule the 15-minute demo for Devesh
Send the Zycus master software agreement to Cadence to start parallel legal review.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Obtain exact counts of contract documents, email records, and any other file types from iManage and Salesforce teams for migration planning
Confirm the go-forward annual contract volume estimate (working figure ~12,500/yr) to finalize pricing.

competitive_position.summary: Two-finalist bake-off: Zycus vs Ironclad, confirmed by the 09 Jun Next Step. Ironclad is the single live threat, strong on legal-AI brand and explicitly admired by Cadence's legal team (23 Jan). The displaced incumbents (Coupa buy-side, homegrown Salesforce/iManage/Word sell-side, DocuSign) are status incumbent/declining. Zycus's edge is enterprise breadth and AI/Merlin intake vs Ironclad's legal-only depth — not yet activated with the budget approver.

competitors.name: Ironclad
competitors.quote: Legal team has pressing AI requirements and has been impressed by tools like iClad
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 23 Jan 2026 + SF Next Step 09 Jun
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the single enterprise platform across procurement + legal + sales with Merlin AI and Teams intake; frame Ironclad as a legal-only point solution leaving procurement/sales fragmented.

competitors.name: Coupa (buy-side CLM incumbent)
competitors.quote: Currently using Coupa for CLM, he's seeking one platform to manage both buy-side vendor contracts and sell-side legal/sales agreements
competitors.how_we_win: Unify buy-side and sell-side on one platform Coupa cannot serve across legal/sales.

competitors.name: Homegrown Salesforce/iManage/Word (sell-side)
competitors.quote: We're starting from scratch, essentially... homegrown, connects or links to Salesforce, links to iManage
competitors.how_we_win: Replace manual, ungoverned intake with orchestrated CLM and AI metadata capture.

competitors.name: DocuSign (e-signature incumbent)
competitors.quote: DocuSign is the de facto e-signature solution
competitors.how_we_win: Integrate with DocuSign rather than displace; not a competitive blocker.

champion_strength.source: SF Next Step (16/26 Jun) + Avoma POC framework call 28 Apr 2026
champion_strength.summary: Best relationship is Peter Hughes, the CLM project lead, who coordinates and provides access but has not advocated for Zycus over Ironclad (no approver intro, no head-to-head POC feedback shared). Treat as a coach to be converted into a champion who will broker the budget-approver intro.

sources: SF Next Step (26 May, leadership budget meeting)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has appeared on any of the 13 deep-read calls. Devesh Chadha (Director - Procurement COE, Decision Maker) controls the RFP, budget conversation and timeline, but he is a decision maker, not the signing economic buyer on a $210k multi-year commitment. The Next Step (26 May) references a Cadence leadership meeting to listen to final budget requests, confirming a leadership/CFO budget layer exists that has not engaged Zycus directly. The 5-year term with annual upfront payment makes that sign-off material. The path to that approver is through the champion, not a cold approach — identifying and getting a 30-minute briefing with the approver, brokered by Peter, is the single biggest power gap.

recommended_moves: Run a 30-minute 1:1 POC debrief with Peter Hughes: learn where Zycus stood vs Ironclad, and have him broker the introduction to the budget approver from the 26 May leadership meeting — do not cold-approach the exec.
Book a legal + procurement redline/SOW sync (Melanie Hamm, Dave Bebb, Jason Lieu) and name a paper owner to drive MSA redlines, SOW and migration scope.

Deliver a 30-minute breadth-led briefing for the budget approver (brokered by Peter), anchored to the ~12,500-contract value case: one platform across procurement + legal + sales with Merlin AI vs Ironclad's legal-only depth.
